Course Title: AI in Automotive Manufacturing: Safety-Critical Decisions
Category: Manufacturing: Chemicals, Food Manufacturing, Industrial Manufacturing, Textiles, Automotive, Pharmaceuticals, Electronics
Who is this for: Manufacturing Engineers, Quality Control Inspectors, Production Supervisors, Plant Managers, Safety Officers, Automotive Technicians, Operations Managers, Process Engineers
Team or Dept this is for: Manufacturing, Quality Assurance, Operations, Production, Safety, Engineering
Description: Impact on parts quality and downstream vehicle safety.
Prerequisites: A basic familiarity with automotive manufacturing processes and an openness to exploring how artificial intelligence tools are being applied in industrial settings are all that is needed to get started with this course.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify how AI is applied within automotive manufacturing processes and explain the potential impact of AI-driven decisions on parts quality and vehicle safety. You will also be able to apply foundational knowledge of safety-critical considerations to recognise the risks and responsibilities associated with using AI tools in an automotive production environment.
